So you have to be careful with this, with discounting your product. If you start to discount your products, the people on your email list will just sit around and wait for your next sale. They won't trust you. Your sale price will become your new normal price. You have to be careful because you just don't always want your products on sale. It's really bad positioning and it just trains your list to wait for the next sale. And you don't want that. ## [09:05 - 12:07]

Or the sales page. Then on day two, you send another email. It's usually related to the story. Maybe it's humorous. Maybe it adds another piece to the story. And most of them were very lighthearted, and poking fun at me. So in this case, your day two email, could be just talk about people writing back to you, and teasing you about the story, or something like that. Day three is the day before you close. So really, you're only open for four days. There's the initial day, where you send that first email, then you're open on day two, where you send some other piece of the story. Then day three is another email that says, hey, the offer ends tomorrow. It's basically a 24 hour warning. And in reality, you're gonna close the next day at midnight, and you're probably going to send this day three email, in the morning. So it's probably more like a 36 hour warning. ## [12:07 - 13:08]

if you've got a list, this is just a great way to bring people in. And another key thing here is, it's not like you're just bringing in money, a chunk of money. You are bringing in a chunk of money, but it's actually you're creating new buyers.
